behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names bash.txt and bash.ts implicitly via 'template file' and 'TypeScript implementation'; all required concepts (maxLines, maxBytes, quoting, specialized tool, timeout/truncation, 20 lines, 100+ words, 5 concepts) are explicitly listed.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the problem (project-specific path placeholder breaks caching) without specifying the exact replacement text or code changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavioral properties (no project-specific paths, identical output across projects, no ': any', exports BashTool) without mandating specific variable names or line positions.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute Node.js subprocesses to compute and compare actual description outputs across simulated projects.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actually removing ${directory} substitution; a stub template wouldn't pass the 20-line/100-word/concept/guidance checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover all stated requirements: no project path substitution, cacheability, global constant substitution, guidance sections, no ': any', BashTool export.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names, docstrings, and section comment headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass).

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies code/text, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names appear consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction references the Bash tool domain without naming specific lines or the ${directory} placeholder identifier.
